summary: A delay in transferring patients to a rehabilitation unit after proximal femur fractures worsens patient outcomes by increasing mortality rates and elevating risks of post-fracture complications such as delirium and prolonged immobility. Timely rehab intervention is critical due to its strong association with improved survival and reduced morbidity.
